title: Automatic stabilizers
description: Practice what you've learned about fiscal policy lags and automatic stabilizers in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
Hamsterville has a progressive income tax and a social insurance program that provides unemployed workers with a basic income while they are searching for jobs.

**What impact will this progressive tax system have on real GDP?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Decrease real GDP during the expansion phase; increase real GDP during the recession phase
Increase real GDP during every phase of the business cycle
Decrease real GDP during all phases of the business cycle
Increase real GDP during the expansion phase; decrease real GDP during the recession phase
We cannot tell the impact taxes will have on real GDP

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes an automatic stabilizer that will help Lincolnland recover from a recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Government transfer programs, such as unemployment benefits
An increase in government spending, such as a new infrastructure project
An increase in income tax revenues
A new law granting tax rebates for firms building new factories
An increase in short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) due to a decrease in the price level

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of an automatic stabilizer that can reduce the effect of a recession on output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Institutions such as paying unemployment compensation
Reserve requirements
Government spending
Laws requiring balanced budgets
One-time tax rebates

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following *best* describes how taxes work as an automatic stabilizer during a recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Tax revenues automatically decrease as GDP falls, which prevents consumption and real GDP from falling further
Tax revenues automatically increase as GDP falls, which prevents consumption and real GDP from falling further
Governments enact new legislation to create tax rebates, which increases consumption and increases real GDP
Governments enact new legislation to create higher taxes, which would increase consumption and decrease real GDP
An automatic decrease in tax revenues causes government spending to decrease, which prevents real GDP from falling further

assessment_items.question_content:
Gross domestic product (GDP) in Marthland is decreasing, plunging Marthaland into a recession.

**How will the response of automatic stabilizers to decreasing GDP in Marthaland affect the economy of Marthaland?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Tax revenues will decrease without governmental action, which will keep consumption and output from falling further.
The government of Marthaland will respond by implementing expansionary fiscal policy, such as a new infrastructure project.
The government will respond by passing a one-time tax cut to stimulate the economy.
The government will implement contractionary fiscal policy, such as decreasing government spending.
Transfer payments will decrease automatically, decreasing spending and output.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the function of automatic stabilizers in an economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They decrease tax revenues when gross domestic product decreases.
They eliminate the business cycle.
They ensure that budgets are balanced.
They increase unemployment during recessions.
They move the economy further away from full-employment output.

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Hamiltonia is experiencing a severe recession. 

**Which of the following actions will occur automatically to increase employment and output without requiring any action by the government of Hamiltonia?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Income tax collected will decrease.
Transfer payments will decrease.
The money supply will increase.
The reserve ratio will increase.
Discretionary government spending increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lincolnland has a progressive income tax and a social insurance program that provides unemployed workers with a basic income while they are searching for jobs. The country experienced a shock and is now producing real GDP equal to Y2, as indicated in the AD-AS model shown here.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best describes what will happen to transfer payments and income taxes as a result of the decrease in AD?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots five labeled lines. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled r G D P. A vertical line labeled L R A S intersects two downward-sloping lines labeled A D 1 and A D 2, and one upward-sloping line labeled S R A S. The intersection of A D 1 and S R A S occurs at price level P L 1 and output Y f. The intersection of A D 2 and S R A S occurs at price level P L 2 and output Y 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from both price levels, and vertical dashed lines extend from both output levels.

choices.content: Transfer payments will increase; taxes will decrease
Transfer payments will increase; taxes will not be affected
Transfer payments will not be affected; taxes will decrease
Transfer payments will decrease; taxes will increase
Taxes and transfer payments will both increase

assessment_items.question_content:
Tax revenues are an example of an automatic stabilizer.

**Which of the following best describes changes in unemployment and real GDP that cause tax revenue to increase?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real GDP is higher than full employment output; unemployment is lower than the natural rate
Real GDP is lower than full employment output; unemployment is lower than the natural rate
Real GDP is higher than full employment output; unemployment is higher than the natural rate of unemployment
Real GDP is higher than full employment output; unemployment is equal to the natural rate
Real GDP is equal to the full employment output; unemployment is less than the natural rate

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do automatic stabilizers affect an economy during inflationary periods?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: As national income increases, tax revenues increase, which slows consumption spending.
Unemployment payments increase, which increases consumption spending.
Governments implement projects and increase government spending.
Governments implement a one-time increase in taxes to reign in consumption.
Central banks sell bonds to decrease the money supply, raising interest rates.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of an automatic stabilizer that helps control inflation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in income tax revenue collected
An increase in the reserve ratio
An increase in discretionary government spending
An increase in the money supply
An increase in spending on unemployment payments
